title: Maughania cumingiana
taxonomy: Maughania cumingiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae, commonly known as Fabaceae. It is a genus of trees and shrubs native to tropical Asia. The classification of Maughania cumingiana is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: angiosperms, clade: eudicots, clade: rosids, order: Fabales, family: Fabaceae, genus: Maughania, species: M. cumingiana. The plant is further divided into various subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. The classification of Maughania cumingiana is essential in understanding its evolution, distribution, and conservation status.

intro: Maughania cumingiana, also known as Maughan's bean, is a climbing plant native to the tropical forests of the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
